data: Estimates reflect the federal "2008 Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans" (available from: http://www.health.gov/PAGuidelines/), which recommend adults perform ‚Č•150 minutes a week of moderate-intensity or ‚Č•75 minutes a week of vigorous-intensity aerobic physical activity, or an equivalent combination, in episodes ‚Č•10 minutes preferably spread throughout the week. The guidelines also recommend that adults perform muscle-strengthening activities of moderate or high intensity involving all major muscle groups on ‚Č•2 days a week. NHIS questions ask about frequency and duration of light-to moderate-intensity and vigorous-intensity leisure-time physical activities, and frequency of leisure-time strengthening activities, phrased in terms of current behavior without a specific reference period.
